questions.question: What does spring-boot-starter-web bring to a project?
options: Spring MVC, embedded Tomcat, Jackson for REST
questions.explanation: starter-web bundles MVC, Jackson JSON, validation API, and embedded servlet container for REST applications.

questions.question: What does @SpringBootApplication combine?
questions.explanation: @SpringBootApplication is a convenience meta-annotation enabling auto-config and component scanning from the main class package.

questions.question: What is spring-boot-starter-data-jpa?
options: Spring Data JPA, Hibernate, JDBC starter for repositories
questions.explanation: It pulls Hibernate as JPA provider, Spring Data repositories, and transaction management for database access.

questions.question: What does JpaRepository<T, ID> extend?
questions.explanation: JpaRepository adds flush, batch delete, and JPA-specific methods atop CrudRepository and PagingAndSortingRepository.

questions.question: How is this method implemented?
options: Spring Data derives query from method name findByLastName
questions.explanation: Query derivation parses findByLastName → WHERE lastName = ?. Custom queries use @Query JPQL or native SQL.

questions.question: What does @Modifying on repository method indicate?
options: UPDATE or DELETE query that changes data; often needs @Transactional
questions.explanation: @Modifying with @Query for UPDATE/DELETE requires clearAutomatically/flush and transactional context.

questions.question: What does @Query(value="...", nativeQuery=true) do?
questions.explanation: nativeQuery=true runs vendor SQL. JPQL (default false) operates on entity names and fields.

questions.question: What is Spring Security filter chain?
options: Ordered servlet filters handling authn/authz before reaching DispatcherServlet
questions.explanation: SecurityFilterChain beans configure filters for session, JWT, CSRF, authorization rules on each request.

questions.question: Where does UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter sit conceptually?
options: Processes login form/basic auth and creates Authentication
questions.explanation: It attempts authentication from credentials and delegates to AuthenticationManager; success stores SecurityContext.

questions.question: What are typical JWT sections?
questions.explanation: JWT is header.algorithm + payload.claims + signature. Server verifies signature with secret/public key.

questions.question: Should sensitive data be stored unencrypted in JWT payload?
options: No—payload is Base64-encoded not encrypted; anyone can read claims
questions.explanation: JWTs are signed for integrity, not confidential by default. Use HTTPS and minimal claims; short expiry.

questions.question: What validates JWT on each request in stateless APIs?
options: OncePerRequestFilter or oauth2ResourceServer JWT decoder in filter chain
questions.explanation: BearerTokenAuthenticationFilter or custom filter parses Authorization: Bearer <token>, validates signature and expiry.
questions.code: // Filter extracts Bearer token, sets SecurityContext

questions.question: What does @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')") require?
questions.explanation: Enable method security (@EnableMethodSecurity). SpEL in @PreAuthorize checks roles/authorities before method runs.

questions.question: Difference between hasRole('USER') and hasAuthority('ROLE_USER')?
options: hasRole adds ROLE_ prefix implicitly; hasAuthority matches exact string
questions.explanation: hasRole('USER') checks ROLE_USER authority. hasAuthority('ROLE_USER') is explicit—know your granted authority strings.

questions.question: What is Authentication vs Authorization?
options: Authentication proves identity; authorization decides access rights
questions.explanation: Authentication (who are you) precedes authorization (what you may do)—roles, scopes, @PreAuthorize rules.

questions.question: What is Netflix Eureka used for in microservices?
options: Service registry: instances register and clients discover service locations
questions.explanation: Eureka server holds service instance metadata; clients heartbeat and fetch registry for load-balanced calls.

questions.question: Which annotation enables Eureka client registration?
questions.explanation: Spring Cloud Netflix uses @EnableDiscoveryClient (preferred) to register with Eureka or other registries.

questions.question: What is OpenFeign in Spring Cloud?
options: Declarative REST client interface with annotations mapping to HTTP calls
questions.explanation: @FeignClient(name="order-service") interface methods become HTTP requests with load balancing via discovery.

questions.question: What does @FeignClient("inventory-service") need at runtime?
options: Service discovery or explicit URL; Feign + LoadBalancer
questions.explanation: Feign resolves service name through Eureka/Consul or url attribute. @EnableFeignClients scans interfaces.

questions.question: What is Spring Cloud Gateway's primary role?
options: API gateway: routing, filtering, cross-cutting concerns at edge
questions.explanation: Gateway routes external requests to internal services, applying predicates/filters (auth, rate limit, rewrite paths).

questions.question: Gateway vs direct client-to-many-services?
options: Gateway always slower with no benefits
Single entry point, centralized SSL, routing, and policies
questions.explanation: Clients call gateway host; gateway load-balances to service instances hiding internal topology.

questions.question: What does CircuitBreaker pattern prevent?
options: Cascading failures by failing fast when downstream is unhealthy
questions.explanation: After failure threshold, circuit opens and calls fail fast or use fallback until half-open retry probes recovery.

questions.question: Which annotation applies circuit breaker in Spring Boot 3 resilience4j?
questions.explanation: @CircuitBreaker on method with resilience4j.spring.boot3 integration—configure failure rate and wait duration in yaml.

questions.question: What is Retry in Resilience4j?
options: Configurable retries with backoff for transient failures
questions.explanation: @Retry attempts failed calls per policy—use only for idempotent operations to avoid duplicate side effects.

questions.question: What is RabbitMQ?
options: Message broker implementing AMQP for async messaging
questions.explanation: Producers publish to exchanges; queues bind and consumers process messages asynchronously for decoupling.

questions.question: Exchange types in RabbitMQ include?
questions.explanation: direct: routing key match; fanout: broadcast; topic: pattern routing; headers: header-based routing.

questions.question: What does @RabbitListener do?
options: Marks method to consume messages from specified queue
questions.explanation: @RabbitListener(queues = "order.created") registers consumer container invoking method per message.

questions.question: What does application.properties spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=validate do?
options: Checks schema matches entities without changing DB
questions.explanation: validate fails startup if mappings mismatch schema—good with Flyway-managed production schema.

questions.question: What is Actuator starter used for?
questions.explanation: spring-boot-starter-actuator exposes /actuator/health and metrics integrated with Micrometer monitoring.

questions.question: What is save() behavior for entity with null id?
options: persist (insert) new entity; non-null id may merge/update
questions.explanation: Spring Data save uses EntityManager persist/merge: new entity inserted, detached existing may merge.

questions.question: In SecurityFilterChain bean, authorizeHttpRequests().anyRequest().authenticated() means?
options: All requests need authenticated user unless other matchers permit
questions.explanation: RequestMatchers can permitAll for /public/**; anyRequest().authenticated() requires logged-in/principal for rest.

questions.question: Where should clients send JWT in HTTP?
questions.explanation: Bearer scheme in Authorization header is standard for stateless APIs. Secure cookies possible with caution (CSRF).

questions.question: What is self-preservation mode in Eureka?
options: Protects registry when many heartbeats fail (network partition) to avoid mass eviction
questions.explanation: When too many instances miss renewals, Eureka assumes network issue and keeps stale entries temporarily.

questions.question: What is a Feign fallback or fallbackFactory?
options: Provides default response when remote call fails (resilience)
questions.explanation: Fallback implements degraded behavior when circuit open or error—return cached data or friendly error.

questions.question: Route predicate Path=/api/orders/** routes to?
options: Configured URI (e.g., lb://order-service) when path matches
questions.explanation: Spring Cloud Gateway RouteLocator defines predicates (Path, Header) and filters forwarding to downstream URIs.

questions.question: Bulkhead pattern purpose?
options: Limit concurrent calls to resource pool preventing thread exhaustion
questions.explanation: Bulkhead isolates thread pools so one slow dependency cannot starve entire application.

questions.question: Why use durable queue?
options: Queue survives broker restart (messages need persistent delivery mode too)
questions.explanation: durable=true on queue declaration survives restart; persistent messages survive with deliveryMode=2.

questions.question: Acknowledgment (ACK) in consuming means?
options: Consumer confirms successful processing; broker removes message
questions.explanation: Manual ack after processing prevents message loss on consumer crash. auto ack risks loss if processing fails after ack.

questions.question: Who can call deleteOrder when annotation is @PreAuthorize("hasAuthority('ORDER_DELETE')")?
questions.explanation: Method security checks granted authorities on Authentication before invoking deleteOrder.

questions.question: What does @Transactional on service class do?
options: Defines transaction boundary; one persistence context per method typically
questions.explanation: Spring @Transactional wraps method in DB transaction—commit on success, rollback on unchecked exceptions per propagation rules.

questions.question: CSRF protection in REST stateless JWT APIs is often?
options: Always required for Bearer token APIs
Disabled when session-less and using tokens; still needed for cookie session apps
questions.explanation: CSRF targets cookie-based sessions. Pure Bearer JWT APIs often disable CSRF; cookie auth must keep protection.

questions.question: Load balancer prefix lb:// in Gateway URI means?
options: Use Spring Cloud LoadBalancer with service discovery
questions.explanation: lb://order-service resolves instances from registry and load-balances requests reactively (WebFlux gateway).
